food_subgroup: Carbonated soft drinks — sugar-free / diet cola

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN0LQ covers diet/sugar-free cola-type soft drinks. No brand-specific Pepsi Black code exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226465004 covers cola drink as a substance category; no sugar-free variant-specific code exists. Closest parent used.

allergens: phenylalanine (contains aspartame — source of phenylalanine, relevant for PKU)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of Pepsi Black, the typical single-serve portion sold in Singapore food establishments and convenience stores.

nutrient_source.title: Pepsi Black (Diet Cola) — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods; cross-referenced with PepsiCo Singapore product label data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Branded Foods) / PepsiCo product labelling
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Pepsi Black is a zero-calorie, zero-sugar cola sweetened with aspartame and acesulfame potassium. Nutrient values per 100 ml (treated as per 100 g for liquid) sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Pepsi Zero Sugar / Pepsi Black, consistent with PepsiCo Singapore product label. Energy, protein, fat, and carbohydrate are all confirmed zero per product formulation and USDA data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — Foster-Powell, Holt & Brand-Miller (2002); University of Sydney GI Database — diet soft drinks
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pepsi Black contains no glycemic carbohydrates (sugar_g = 0.0, carbohydrate_g = 0.0). Diet cola beverages are classified as having a negligible glycemic index. The University of Sydney GI Database lists diet soft drinks as effectively zero-GI. Per the zero-calorie beverage glycemic convention required by Veritas v2, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) and gi_category is set to 'low'. Glycemic load per 330 ml serving = (1 × 0.0) / 100 = 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (11 mg per 100 ml; ~36 mg per 330 ml can)
hypertension.guidance: Pepsi Black contains minimal sodium (~36 mg per 330 ml can), posing negligible direct sodium load for hypertensive individuals; however, regular consumption of caffeinated beverages may transiently elevate blood pressure, and patients on antihypertensive medications should be aware of caffeine interactions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; artificial sweeteners (aspartame, acesulfame potassium)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pepsi Black contains no sugar or glycemic carbohydrates and does not raise postprandial blood glucose; however, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that habitual consumption of artificially sweetened beverages has been associated in some observational studies with altered insulin sensitivity and sweet-taste dysregulation, and moderation is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero sugar, caffeine content (~34 mg per 330 ml)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Pepsi Black contributes no dietary fat, cholesterol, or sugar, making it neutral with respect to lipid and glycaemic cardiovascular risk factors; however, the caffeine content (~34 mg per 330 ml) may be relevant for individuals with arrhythmias or those advised to limit stimulant intake, and clinicians should counsel accordingly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pepsi Black (also marketed as Pepsi Zero Sugar or Pepsi Max in some markets) is a sugar-free, zero-calorie carbonated cola beverage produced by PepsiCo. It is sweetened with a blend of aspartame and acesulfame potassium (Ace-K) in place of sucrose or high-fructose corn syrup, delivering the characteristic cola taste without glycemic carbohydrates. The beverage is dark brown in colour, highly carbonated, and has a crisp, slightly bitter cola flavour profile with a clean finish compared to regular Pepsi. In Singapore, Pepsi Black is widely available at food courts, hawker centres, fast-food outlets, and convenience stores, often sold in 330 ml cans or as a fountain drink. It is a popular choice for calorie-conscious consumers and individuals managing blood glucose levels. Nutritionally, Pepsi Black provides essentially zero energy, zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrate per 100 ml, with a small amount of sodium (~11 mg/100 ml) and phosphorus (~29 mg/100 ml) from its acidulant (phosphoric acid). The caffeine content is approximately 10 mg per 100 ml (~34 mg per 330 ml can). It contains phenylalanine as a metabolic product of aspartame and carries a phenylketonuria (PKU) warning. The glycemic index is effectively zero, making it suitable for diabetic dietary plans when consumed in moderation. Clinically, it is considered low concern for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease, though habitual consumption of artificially sweetened beverages warrants monitoring in clinical nutrition contexts.

confidence_reason: Pepsi Black is a globally standardised branded product with a fixed formulation. Nutrient values are confirmed via USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods and are consistent with PepsiCo Singapore product labelling. All core macros are confirmed zero by product formulation. GI is effectively zero by absence of glycemic carbohydrates, consistent with University of Sydney GI Database classification of diet soft drinks.
notes: 1. energy_kcal is confirmed zero: Pepsi Black is a zero-calorie beverage by formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods. 2. protein_g is confirmed zero: contains no protein by formulation. 3. fat_g is confirmed zero: contains no fat by formulation. 4. carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero: all sweetness is derived from non-glycemic artificial sweeteners (aspartame, Ace-K); confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 5. sugar_g is confirmed zero: no added or intrinsic sugars present. 6. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero: naturally zero for a carbonated beverage with no plant-derived fibre. 7. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by formulation. 8. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for this product. 9. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 10. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 11. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 12. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 13. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 14. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 15. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 16. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 17. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 18. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 19. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 20. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 21. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 22. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. 23. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. 24. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the product is served in a Singapore food establishment context. 25. Pepsi Black contains aspartame; individuals with phenylketonuria (PKU) must avoid this product. 26. The cuisine field reflects the serving context (Singapore food establishment) rather than a cuisine of origin; region_of_origin is United States as the brand originates from PepsiCo USA.
